purpose: Document the lineage of BERU's synthetic training corpus to satisfy beru-design-decisions.md D-005 (synthetic-only training data) and AI RMF MAP 2.2 (training data and provenance documented). Required reading before any fine-tune run.

policy_decisions.D-005: Synthetic-only training data. No real client SSPs, scanner output, or PII.
policy_decisions.D-010: Promotion gate requires post-fine-tune evals to beat baseline scores recorded in 5-experiments/exp-005-beru-3b-baseline/.

chatml_corpus.format: ChatML JSONL — one example per line, messages array with system/user/assistant roles

generation_source.model: Hand-authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (instructor agent) per CAPSTONE-PROJECT/beru-design-decisions.md D-012
generation_source.generated_by: Claude Code instructor sessions, validated by project lead
generation_source.generation_method: Manual authoring with contrast-pair structure: each unsafe authority-bypass scenario paired with a near-twin legitimate scenario sharing similar RAG context
generation_source.rejected_predecessor: _archived-200-rejected.jsonl (200 examples, all 200 with wrong control-name pairings, see chatml-examples/README.md)

chatml_corpus.validation_status: passes 8-tests/test_beru_training_data.py (25 tests, 1 skipped) as of 2026-05-08; control-name pairings 100% correct against canonical NIST 800-53 names from GP-CONSULTING/NIST-800-53/controls/*.md frontmatter
chatml_corpus.intended_use: LoRA fine-tuning of llama3.2:3b base into beru:v1.0 challenger. r=32, alpha=64, 2 epochs, target_modules=q_proj,v_proj.
out_of_scope: Real client scanner output (forbidden by D-005)
PII or PHI of any kind (forbidden by D-005)
Examples that hallucinate control IDs (caught by test_beru_training_data.py)

synthetic_ssps.format: Markdown — one SSP per file, quality-suffix in filename (bad/mediocre/good)
synthetic_ssps.purpose: Train BERU's SSP-quality discrimination — bad (vague 'organization implements'), mediocre (named tooling), good (specific control + tool + last review date + evidence path)
synthetic_ssps.intended_use: RAG retrieval target for SSP quality examples; possible future fine-tune signal for SSP narrative authoring.

intake_samples.format: Markdown — AI System Registration forms (per AI RMF GOVERN 1.1)
intake_samples.purpose: Train BERU's recognition of intake-form completeness — represent 5 fictional AI systems (Atlas-v1, Apex-v2, BERU-v4, Nimbus-v1, Orion-v3) at varying completeness levels
intake_samples.note_on_beru_v4: ai-intake-04.md describes a fictional 'BERU-v4' synthetic system. The model field was updated 2026-05-08 from 'Llama 3.1-8B' to 'Llama 3.2-3B' per D-009 to keep training samples consistent with the real BERU's base model — this prevents BERU learning that 8B is a normal answer for itself.

rejected_corpus.format: ChatML JSONL (archived, NOT used for training)
rejected_corpus.rejection_reason: 200/200 examples had wrong control-name pairings; cookie-cutter remediation/risk/evidence-gap strings; zero adversarial examples. See CAPSTONE-PROJECT/beru-design-decisions.md D-012.
rejected_corpus.kept_for: Audit traceability and as a teaching artifact for 'what bad synthetic data looks like'

nist_800_53.SR-3 (Supply Chain Controls): Synthetic origin documented above; no public-hub downloads.
nist_800_53.SR-4 (Provenance): Generation source, model, operator, and date recorded for every artifact.
nist_800_53.SI-7 (Information Integrity): SHA-256 hash recorded for every file. Drift detectable.
nist_800_53.PT-1 (PII Processing Policy): Synthetic-only policy eliminates PII processing in training pipeline.

nist_ai_rmf.MAP 4.1 (Component risks mapped): Training data is a third-party component (Gemini-generated). Risks listed in 'out_of_scope' above.
nist_ai_rmf.MEASURE 2.10 (Privacy): Synthetic data is privacy-safe by construction.

owasp_llm_top_10.LLM03 (Training Data Poisoning): Synthetic-only sourcing reduces external poisoning surface. Pre-train data quality gate at 8-tests/test_beru_training_data.py.

drift_detection.rule: If any artifact's SHA-256 changes without a corresponding manifest update, that is a CM-3 (Configuration Change Control) violation. Fine-tune MUST NOT run against drifted data.
